logic: The LUF Requirements function as part of a pathway to certification within Gold Standard for the Global Goals, enabling eligible forestry and agriculture activities to undergo Design and Performance Certification.

summary: Conjunction with P&R and Product Requirements
logic: LUF Projects shall apply these Requirements in conjunction with the Principles & Requirements and GHG Emissions Reduction & Sequestration Product Requirements to issue GS VERs.

A/R projects can include: planting trees, single-species plantations, all silvicultural systems (conservation forests, selective harvesting, rotation forestry), and agriculture (agroforestry) or pasture (silvopasture).
AGR projects include eligible activities covered by an approved Gold Standard SDG Impact Quantification methodology.
AGR projects shall hold FSC certification valid throughout the crediting period.
source_span_text: AGR projects shall hold FSC certification valid throughout the crediting period. FSC certification report required.

expected_evidence.label: FSC Certificate
expected_evidence.description: Valid FSC dual certification certificate

expected_evidence.label: FSC Certification Report
expected_evidence.description: FSC certification report document

conditions: FSC certification valid throughout crediting period
exceptions: FSC certification lapses → project not eligible for GS VERs

logic: Project Developer shall: own CO2 user rights, hold uncontested legal land title, own timber/non-timber forest product rights, hold all necessary permits.
source_span_text: For the duration of the crediting period the Project Developer shall: where a Gold Standard Certified Statement or Product (e.g. GSVERs) is sought, Project Developers must own the CO2 user rights or carbon sequestration rights for the project area, AND hold an uncontested legal land title for the Project Area, AND own the rights for timber and non-timber forest products for the project area, AND hold all necessary permits to implement the project (planting permits, infrastructure permits, harvesting permits, etc.)

expected_evidence.label: CO2 User Rights Documentation
expected_evidence.description: Legal document proving PD owns carbon sequestration rights

expected_evidence.label: Uncontested Legal Land Title
expected_evidence.description: Land title documentation valid for crediting period

expected_evidence.label: Timber and Non-Timber Forest Product Rights
expected_evidence.description: Documentation of harvesting and product rights

expected_evidence.label: Implementation Permits
expected_evidence.description: Planting, infrastructure, and harvesting permits

conditions: CO2 user rights owned by PD for crediting period duration
Legal land title uncontested and documented
Timber and non-timber forest product rights owned
All necessary implementation permits held

summary: AGR secured titles - developer on behalf
logic: Where PD acts on behalf: each participant signs agreement confirming CO2 user rights passed to PD, rights to implement activities, uncontested land title.

summary: AGR secured titles - developer acts alone
logic: Where PD acts alone: PD holds CO2 user rights, rights to implement activities, uncontested land title.
New areas added after Registered status via Design Certification process. VVB site visit and opinion required.
If host country accounts for land-based activities nationally, projects must conform to GHG Product Requirements Annex A Double Counting.
source_span_text: If the host country accounts for greenhouse-gas (GHG) emissions, and hereby accounts for its own land-based activities under its national or subnational accounting, then projects seeking GSVERs shall conform to the GHG Emissions Reduction and Sequestration Product Requirements - Annex A Double Counting Requirements.

expected_evidence.label: Host Country NDC Status
expected_evidence.description: Evidence of whether host country includes LULUCF in NDC

expected_evidence.label: Double Counting Assessment
expected_evidence.description: Assessment per GHG Product Requirements Annex A

conditions: Host country accounts for land-based activities nationally/subnationally
exceptions: Host country does not include LULUCF in national accounting

logic: Preserving adaptive capacity integral to every AGR project. Demonstrated via training, knowledge sharing, livelihood diversification, soil fertility improvement.
PD conducts Safeguarding Principles assessment per Risks & Capacities Guideline. Submitted Preliminary Review, updated for Design and Performance Certification.
source_span_text: The Project Developer shall conduct the Safeguarding Principles assessment following Safeguarding Principles & Requirements and Risks & Capacities Guideline assessed for the Project Area, taking into account likely issues in the context of the Project Region. The assessment outcome shall be submitted for Preliminary Review and updated as required for Design Certification and Performance Certification.

expected_evidence.label: Safeguarding Principles Assessment
expected_evidence.description: Completed assessment per Safeguarding P&R and R&C Guideline

expected_evidence.label: Risks and Capacities Assessment
expected_evidence.description: Risks & Capacities Guideline assessment for project area

conditions: Assessment conducted per Safeguarding Principles & Requirements
Risks & Capacities Guideline applied to Project Area
Project Region context considered
Submitted for Preliminary Review
Updated for Design and Performance Certification

logic: Minimum 10% of total Project Area for biodiversity protection following HCV approach: native tree patches, solitary stems, endangered species habitats, connectivity areas.
source_span_text: A minimum of 10% of the total Project Area shall be identified and used to protect or enhance the biological diversity following High Conservation Value (HCV) approach. The designated protected area shall be located within the Project Area and managed by the Project Developer. The protected area can include buffer zone areas for water bodies.

expected_evidence.label: Biodiversity Protection Area Map
expected_evidence.description: GIS map showing 10%+ area designated for biodiversity

expected_evidence.label: HCV Assessment
expected_evidence.description: High Conservation Value assessment identifying native species, habitats, connectivity

expected_evidence.label: Biodiversity Management Plan
expected_evidence.description: Plan for managing the protected area within project

conditions: Minimum 10% of total Project Area designated
Located within Project Area boundaries
Managed by Project Developer
Follows HCV approach
Identifies: native patches, solitary stems, endangered habitats, connectivity areas
exceptions: Buffer zones for water bodies can count toward 10%

logic: 15-meter buffer on both sides of water bodies. In buffer: keep native trees, no fertilizer/pesticides, no logging, no heavy machinery, no cropping. Planted trees must be native.
source_span_text: The Project Developer shall maintain a buffer zone of 15 meters for water bodies on both sides of any permanent or temporary water bodies such as lakes, streams, rivers, wetlands, etc. Irrigation channels are excluded from this requirement. In these buffer zones: (a) All existing native trees shall be kept, AND (b) No fertilizer and pesticides shall be used, AND (c) No logging activities shall take place, AND (d) No heavy machinery shall be used, AND (e) No cropping is allowed, AND (f) In case trees are being planted, these need to be native tree species.

expected_evidence.label: Water Body Map
expected_evidence.description: GIS map showing all permanent and temporary water bodies with 15m buffer zones

expected_evidence.label: Buffer Zone Compliance Records
expected_evidence.description: Monitoring records confirming no prohibited activities in buffer zones

conditions: 15-meter buffer maintained on both sides
Existing native trees kept in buffer
No fertilizer/pesticides in buffer
No logging in buffer
No heavy machinery in buffer
No cropping in buffer
Planted trees must be native species
exceptions: Irrigation channels excluded from buffer requirement

logic: Stakeholder Consultation before project start date. Refer to Stakeholder Consultation Engagement Requirements.
source_span_text: The Stakeholder Consultation shall be conducted prior to the project start date. The Project Developer shall refer to Stakeholder Consultation Engagement Requirements for further details.

expected_evidence.label: Stakeholder Consultation Report
expected_evidence.description: Completed consultation report per SC Engagement Requirements

expected_evidence.label: Consultation Timing Evidence
expected_evidence.description: Evidence consultation occurred before project start date

conditions: Stakeholder Consultation completed before project start date

logic: AGR: first within 2 years. A/R: every 5 years. All: crediting renewal every 5th year with baseline update.
source_span_text: AGR specific: The first Verification shall be completed either within two years of project start date or Project Design Certification, whichever is later. A/R specific: Verification shall be completed at least every 5 years until the end of the crediting period. According to the Principles & Requirements, all projects shall seek Crediting Period Renewal every 5th year.

expected_evidence.label: Verification Schedule
expected_evidence.description: Documented verification schedule meeting frequency requirements

expected_evidence.label: Baseline Update at Renewal
expected_evidence.description: Updated baseline per Impact Quantification Methodology at 5-year renewal

conditions: AGR: first verification within 2 years of start or Design Certification (whichever later)
A/R: verification at least every 5 years
All: crediting period renewal every 5th year with baseline update

logic: Option 1: Apply A/R CDM Combined tool for baseline and additionality. CDM terms interpreted in Gold Standard context.
Option 1: Apply CDM standardized baselines guidelines or Combined tool.
Option 2: Must meet LDC/low HDI, not mandatory, degraded land, plus one of: native species, community benefit, 5+ species in 50%+ area, HDI<0.5 or SIDS.
Option 2: HDI<=0.7, not mandatory/enforced, plus one of: <600mm rain, 5+ native crop species, smallholder <5% adoption, HDI<0.5 or SIDS.
Maximum 3 years retroactive, from Project Start Date or 3 years before Design Certification, whichever is later.
All projects submit GIS layers: Project Region, Project Area, Eligible Areas, Modelling Units, Infrastructure, Land use, Water bodies, Carbon pools.
source_span_text: All projects shall submit GIS vector layers (Table 4). The GIS vector layers shall be labelled comprehensively: Project Region, Project Area, Eligible Areas, Individual Modelling Units, Infrastructure (road, houses, etc.), Land use, Water bodies, Carbon pools.

expected_evidence.label: GIS Vector Layers
expected_evidence.description: Complete set of labelled GIS layers per Table 4

conditions: All required GIS layers submitted
Layers comprehensively labelled
Includes: Project Region, Project Area, Eligible Areas, Modelling Units, Infrastructure, Land use, Water bodies, Carbon pools

logic: A/R: 1 GPS per 5ha with hand-drawn map. AGR: 1 GPS per participant with map. Protected areas via UNESCO/national park maps.
Target precision: 20% of mean at 90% confidence level for all GHG estimates.
notes: See GS LUF v1.2.1 Section A.
source_span_text: Estimated greenhouse gas emissions and removals resulting from Land Use and Forestry (LUF) activities have uncertainties associated with the measurements/estimates of various parameters, especially area or other activity data, carbon stocks, biomass growth rates, expansion factors, emission factors and other coefficients. This guideline provides a step-by-step approach on how to treat uncertainties in LUF projects and how to comply with the required target precision of 20% of the mean at a 90% confidence level.

expected_evidence.label: Uncertainty Analysis
expected_evidence.description: Quantified uncertainty for all key parameters showing compliance with 20% threshold

expected_evidence.label: Approach Documentation
expected_evidence.description: Documentation of which approach (1, 2, or 3) was used and justification

conditions: Uncertainty of estimated GHG reductions is ≤20% of mean at 90% confidence

logic: Approach 1: on-site measurements. Approach 2: peer-reviewed literature. Approach 3: default factors with deduction if threshold not met.
notes: See GS LUF v1.2.1 Section A.

logic: If uncertainty >20%: 20-30% → 50% UD, 30-40% → 75% UD, 40-50% → 100% UD. No credits if >50%.
notes: See GS LUF v1.2.1 Section A.
source_span_text: If the uncertainty is greater than 20% of the mean value, then the project owner shall use the estimated value subject to an Uncertainty Deduction (UD). Table: 20<U≤30% → 50% UD, 30<U≤40% → 75% UD, 40<U≤50% → 100% UD.

expected_evidence.label: Uncertainty Deduction Calculation
expected_evidence.description: Calculation showing UD applied per Table 1

conditions: If uncertainty 20-30%: 50% deduction applied
If uncertainty 30-40%: 75% deduction applied
If uncertainty 40-50%: 100% deduction applied
exceptions: Uncertainty >50%: no credits issued for that period
